strengths: Two-stage framework (Teacher-Student) effectively bridges history-conditioned RL and efficient deployment.
History-conditioned approach provides a robust solution for dynamics adaptation without per-domain retraining.
Strong experimental validation on Unitree H1 robot demonstrating zero-shot transfer under unseen conditions.
Online distillation enables a compact and efficient student policy for real-world deployment.
weaknesses: Reliance on a specific robot morphology (Humanoid) might limit immediate generalization to other platforms.
The ablation study on history length could be more extensive to prove the sufficiency of the trajectory window.
The complexity of the two-stage training process might increase the computational cost and training instability.
notes: HoRD presents a well-motivated and technically sound approach to robust humanoid control. The combination of history-conditioned RL and online distillation is innovative and addresses a critical challenge in sim-to-real transfer. The experimental results on a real-world humanoid robot are compelling, showing significant improvements in handling dynamics shifts and external perturbations. The paper is well-written and clearly structured, though some details in the distillation process could be further elaborated.
